headword_jp: {遠|えん}{距|きょ}{離|り}{攻|こう}{撃|げき}

definitions.gloss_en: an attack performed at range, without physical contact with the target
definitions.gloss_jp: {目|もく}{標|ひょう}と{直|ちょく}{接|せつ}{接|せっ}{触|しょく}せずに{行|おこな}う{攻|こう}{撃|げき}（{弓|ゆみ}、{銃|じゅう}、{魔|ま}{法|ほう}など）
definitions.explanation_en: An attack that does not require the attacker to be adjacent to the target. Includes bows, firearms, spells, and other projectile or indirect attacks.
definitions.implicit_notes: Contrasts structurally with 近接攻撃 (melee attack). 遠距離 (far distance) is a general Japanese term used in transportation, telecommunications, and sports as well as games.
definitions.orthography_notes: 遠距離: 遠 (far) + 距離 (distance). 攻撃: attack. The compound is descriptive, not proprietary. Goo dictionary lists 遠距離 as a standard word. Fire Emblem uses the 遠距離 vs. 近接 distinction as a core weapon-type concept.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Cross-genre applicable. The docx confirms this term has independent Fire Emblem precedent. In miniature wargames, applies to shooting weapons. Not WH-specific.

usage_jp: {遠|えん}{距|きょ}{離|り}{攻|こう}{撃|げき}を{行|おこな}う (perform a ranged attack); {遠|えん}{距|きょ}{離|り}{武|ぶ}{器|き} (ranged weapon)
comparative_notes: 近接攻撃 is the antonym. In Fire Emblem, 遠距離 weapon vs. 近接 weapon is a fundamental dichotomy. In miniature wargames, 遠距離攻撃 happens in the 射撃段階.

examples.japanese: {射|しゃ}{撃|げき}{段|だん}{階|かい}に{遠|えん}{距|きょ}{離|り}{攻|こう}{撃|げき}を{実|じっ}{行|こう}する。
examples.english: Execute a ranged attack during the shooting phase.

cross_references.note: 射撃段階 — the phase in which ranged attacks occur
